notes: Jedi Academy has a native source port that can be found at https://github.com/JACoders/OpenJK
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Jedi Academy™

notes:
Needs "__GL_ExtensionStringVersion=17700 %command%" launch option to start.

The game is also old and doesn't properly support widescreen resolutions, but you can force it via the in-game console (you only have to do it once, the game remembers the settings) for my UHD display I ran these commands (Press shift+~ to open the console)


r_customwidth 3840
r_customheight 2160
set cg_fov 110 (the default FOV is too low, this also helps with the mouse sensitivity, which feels too high with the default settings)
r_mode -1
vid_restart (allows you to apply the settings without a restart)

After this the experience is perfect.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Jedi Academy™

notes: Crashes as soon as I start it.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Jedi Academy™

gpu: X.Org AMD BARTS PRO [Radeon HD 6850] (DRM 2.50.0 / 4.15.0-44-generic, LLVM 7.0.0)
notes: Runs with following launch option: "MESA_EXTENSION_MAX_YEAR=2003 %command%".(for AMD Cards) If the game doesn´t fill up your screen and you have a 1600x900 Monitor like me then open the ingame console with SHIFT+^(not ctrl key. It is the one upon the tab key.) and type r_customwidth 1600, r_customheight 900, r_mode -1 and vid_restart one by one. Then it should be fine.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Jedi Academy™

notes:
Works fine after adding “__GL_ExtensionStringVersion=17700 %command%” to the launch options in Steam. If you are like me and have a modern screen resolution (mine is 1440p) Also do the following commands as previously provided by others.
Enter them by opening the console (Shift+~) then typing each line
r_customwidth 2560
r_customheight 1440 
set cg_fov 90
r_mode -1 vid_restart
Then restart the game.

title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Jedi Academy™

notes: Ran even better than I remember it doing under Windows. No tweaks necessary to make it run. Only issue is that it doesn't expose a full set of screen resolutions when configuring from in-game, so you have to edit the config file by hand to get 1920x1080. You have to do that under Windows as well, so I can't penalize Proton for that.
